title: Official Kickoff & Welcome
description: Registration, welcome from organizers, and the official Agentic AI Build Week kickoff. Get oriented, meet your venue hosts, and set your week's intent. [MOCK]

title: Render the Next Era of Creation with the BytePlus AI Stack
description: Hands-on with the BytePlus AI stack for multimodal generation. Render, model orchestration, and creation pipelines you can use the rest of the week.

title: Building Agents with Claude: A Practical Kickoff
description: Intro to building agents with Claude. Tool use, structured output, prompt patterns. Bring a laptop, leave with a running agent. [MOCK]

title: OpenAI Agents SDK Overview
description: Tour of the OpenAI Agents SDK. Tool calling, handoffs, structured output. End with a working multi-step agent. [MOCK]

title: Inside the NVIDIA Inception Program: How Startups Build and Scale AI Globally
description: How early-stage AI startups join NVIDIA Inception and scale globally. Compute credits, GTM, technical mentorship.

title: TRAE in Your Professional Workflow
description: Practical patterns for using TRAE in your daily coding workflow. Beyond autocomplete: refactors, multi-file edits, and agent-assisted shipping.

title: Deploying AI Apps on Vercel
description: How to ship AI apps that don't fall over. Streaming responses, edge functions, observability, cost guardrails. [MOCK]

title: Vector Search Fundamentals
description: Vector embeddings, similarity search, and using MongoDB Atlas Vector Search for your first RAG pipeline. [MOCK]

title: Community Welcome Mixer
description: Meet builders, find teammates, swap ideas. Light food and drinks. Bring your idea or come browse other builders'. [MOCK]

title: Late-Night Vibe Coding
description: Open coworking. Heads-down building with other builders. Coffee, snacks, late-night energy. [MOCK]

title: Claude Tool Use: Patterns That Actually Work
description: Deep dive on tool calling with Claude. Multi-tool routing, parallel calls, error recovery, schema design that the model respects. [MOCK]

title: Realtime API Workshop: Voice Agents in 90 Minutes
description: Build a voice agent from scratch with the OpenAI Realtime API. Microphone capture, function calling, interruption handling. [MOCK]

title: Streaming Responses & Edge Functions
description: Hands-on streaming token-by-token responses, server actions, and AI SDK patterns. Ship a chat UI by lunch. [MOCK]

title: LangGraph for Stateful Agents
description: Build stateful multi-step agents with LangGraph. Branching, checkpoints, human-in-the-loop. Walk away with a working graph. [MOCK]

title: Atlas Vector Search for Production RAG
description: Move past toy RAG demos. Indexing strategy, hybrid search, chunking that works, and eval-driven iteration on Atlas. [MOCK]

title: Building MCP Servers
description: Model Context Protocol from scratch. Tools, resources, prompts. Ship an MCP server you can plug into Claude Desktop by the end. [MOCK]

title: GPU Compute for AI Workloads on Modal
description: Serverless GPU primitives. Deploy a fine-tuned model, run batch inference, schedule background jobs. [MOCK]

title: Workers AI: Edge Inference at Scale
description: Run inference at the edge with Cloudflare Workers AI. Latency, R2 storage, Vectorize, AI Gateway. [MOCK]

title: Production Agents with Mastra
description: Mastra agent framework end-to-end. Workflows, memory, RAG, evals, deployment. [MOCK]

title: Partner Mixer & Networking
description: Drinks with partners and mentors. Office hours, perk pickup, casual conversations. [MOCK]

title: Build, Deploy and Monetize AI Agents: The Future of the Developer Economy
description: Apify walk-through: build agents, deploy them, and monetize via the Apify marketplace. Real talk on the developer economy emerging around agents.

title: Eval Loops & Test Harnesses for Agents
description: Eval-driven agent development. Golden datasets, regression suites, judge prompts, ship-blocking gates. [MOCK]

title: Payments for AI Agents
description: Let your agent take money. Stripe Checkout, subscriptions, usage-based billing, refund flows the agent can actually execute. [MOCK]

title: Multi-Agent System Architecture
description: When to split into multiple agents. Orchestrator vs. swarm, shared state, hand-off patterns, failure modes. [MOCK]

title: Agent Memory Patterns
description: Short-term, episodic, semantic memory. What to persist, what to throw away, how to retrieve at scale. [MOCK]

title: Beyond Autocomplete: How Agentic AI Solves the Enterprise Design Bottleneck
description: Google Developers on agentic AI for enterprise design workflows. Beyond suggestion-style assistance, into agents that ship real artifacts.

title: TRAE in Team Workflows
description: How small teams collaborate with TRAE: shared context, PR-driven flows, review loops. Build a workflow your team can ship in a week. [MOCK]

title: Vision Models & Computer Use
description: Use vision models to read screens, parse documents, and drive browsers. Pixel-level prompting, latency tradeoffs. [MOCK]

title: Observability for AI Agents
description: Traces, spans, evals in production. Catch hallucinations and tool-call failures before users do. [MOCK]

title: Community Night
description: Builders, partners, mentors. Mid-week breather: drinks, music, dinner. Show what you've built so far. [MOCK]

title: Clinic: Tool-Use Debugging
description: Bring your broken agent. Anthropic engineers debug live: failing tool calls, schema mismatches, infinite loops. [MOCK]

title: Clinic: Eval Design
description: OpenAI engineers help you design evals that catch real regressions. Bring your prompt and your shipping risk. [MOCK]

title: Clinic: Deploy & Observability
description: Ship your agent live with Vercel engineers. Streaming, edge, error tracking, cost monitoring. [MOCK]

title: Mentor Drop-In Office Hours
description: 30-min slots with senior builders, founders, and partner engineers. Sign up in the AABW Discord. [MOCK]

title: Demo Pitch Coaching
description: Pitch dry-run with coaches. Story arc, demo flow, what judges are listening for. 5 minutes of feedback per team. [MOCK]

title: Judging Rubric Walkthrough
description: Judges walk through the rubric: technical bar, originality, agentic depth, demo quality. Q&A. [MOCK]

title: Partner Quick-Fire Q&A
description: Rapid-fire Q&A with every partner sponsor. 5 minutes each. Get unstuck before the all-nighter. [MOCK]

title: AI Night
description: Talks, demos, food. The big evening event before Demo Day. Lightning talks from partners and top teams. [MOCK]

title: Late-Night Building (Overnight)
description: Heads-down build session, runs through the night. Coffee, energy drinks, snacks. Last push before Demo Day. [MOCK]

title: Demo Day Dress Rehearsal
description: Full run-through of your demo on stage. AV check, timing, slot order. Last chance to fix what'll break. [MOCK]

title: Final Submission Window
description: Lock your submission. Code freeze, video upload, Devpost finalization. Help desk on-site. [MOCK]

title: Demo Day Presentations
description: Each team presents to judges and the audience. 5-min demo + 2-min Q&A. The whole week pays off here. [MOCK]

title: Judging Round
description: Judges deliberate while remaining demos finish. Optional 1:1 follow-ups with judges. [MOCK]

title: Awards & Closing Ceremony
description: Awards, partner shoutouts, closing remarks. The official end of AABW. After-party kicks off right after. [MOCK]
